Craig — thanks for connecting. Noticed something about the Beverley Sports & Leisure online presence worth mentioning. Happy to share if it's useful.

strategy_reference.note: Retained for Batch 6 reference. The note strategy below applies to future batches — not current active leads.
strategy_reference.tone: Slightly awkward genuine observation — specific and imperfect. Stands out from polished AI slop by being concrete and mildly unpolished.
anti_patterns: Do not use 'I came across your profile'
Do not use 'I was impressed by' or 'I love what you do'
Do not mention AI, automation, digital marketing, web design, or SEO explicitly
Do not pitch in the note — curiosity and connection only
Do not use 'reach out', 'touch base', or 'synergies'
Do not reference the email sequence, SSL, mobile, 'Not Secure', website audit, or any prior contact
what_works: Start with their name — personal, cuts through noise
Name the specific business — shows real attention
One specific observation — not vague, not flattering, just real
End with low-pressure framing: 'Good to connect', 'Connecting in case useful'
Slightly awkward construction is fine — reads human, not bot
Post-reference is always preferred when available — 3-4x higher acceptance

day53_templates_archive.note: Full sector templates from the Day 53 final archive run (2026-05-19) are preserved below for Batch 6 reference. They cover all Ryedale sectors — the same sectors apply in Harrogate/Craven with different town names. NOT ACTIVE — ARCHIVED REFERENCE ONLY.

post_reference_any_sector.template: [First name] — saw your post about [post topic in 4-6 words]. [One honest specific observation about the post, 5-8 words]. Good to connect. [Your first name]
post_reference_any_sector.example_1: James — saw your post about rural broadband in Ryedale. The infrastructure point rang true. Good to connect. Chris
post_reference_any_sector.example_2: Sarah — saw your post about the Helmsley autumn food festival. Looks like a great turnout. Good to connect. Chris

solicitors_post_reference.template: [First name] — saw your post about [legal topic / local charity / community event]. [Firm name] stood out looking at professional services in [town]. Good to connect. [Your first name]
solicitors_post_reference.example_harrogate: David — saw your post about the new leasehold reform changes. Harrogate & Co Solicitors stood out looking at professional services in Harrogate. Good to connect. Chris

solicitors_generic.template: [First name] — [Firm name] stood out looking at professional services in [town]. Good to see an established local firm. Would be good to connect. [Your first name]
solicitors_generic.example_harrogate: David — Harrogate Law stood out looking at professional services in Harrogate. Good to see an established local firm. Would be good to connect. Chris

trades_generic.template: [First name] — [Business name] came up when looking at [trade] businesses in [town]. Noticed something about the online presence. Connecting in case it's useful. [Your first name]
trades_generic.example_harrogate: James — Harrogate Plumbing came up when looking at plumbing businesses in Harrogate. Noticed something about the online presence. Connecting in case it's useful. Chris
trades_generic.skip_rule: If no confirmed profile: mark skip_reason: PROFILE_NOT_FOUND in profiles.json. Do not invent a match.

if_over_200: Remove county — 'North Yorkshire' is 15 chars. Use '[town]' alone.
'Connecting in case it's useful' → 'Good to connect' (saves 16 chars)
'when I was looking at' → 'looking at' (saves 11 chars)
Trim 'Good to see [X] doing well in North Yorkshire' → 'Good to connect' (saves ~30 chars)
Shorten post observation to 4-5 words if needed
Remove your first name at the end — gains 6-10 chars

campaign_linkedin_assessment.root_cause: HANDS execution latency — LinkedIn window missed for every batch across all 59 campaign days. Notes generated correctly by BRAIN every session. Zero notes confirmed sent by HANDS.
campaign_linkedin_assessment.lesson: BRAIN note generation is not the bottleneck. HANDS delivery is. orchestrator.py solves this by making delivery same-session and automated. No Batch 6 LinkedIn without it.
campaign_linkedin_assessment.pipeline_written_off_total: £800-1,175+ (warm pipeline leads 1/3/22) + Batch 5 Ryedale (unquantified — 0 confirmed replies)
